commands.review_summary:
The branch narrows diagnostics OTel protocol config to `http/protobuf`, adds doctor compatibility migration/tests for legacy `grpc` configs, updates OTel docs, and currently carries merge-refresh formatting/import-order churn across unrelated files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows config types/schema/help still accept or advertise `grpc`, while diagnostics-otel returns before OTLP exporter startup unless the protocol is `http/protobuf`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the shipped-config migration semantics plus current-head check completion, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Reject unsupported diagnostics OTel grpc config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93069.

This keeps the config contract honest without stranding existing configs:

- `diagnostics.otel.protocol` now documents and validates the only implemented transport: `http/protobuf`.
- Existing shipped configs with `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` are now handled by `openclaw doctor --fix` instead of simply becoming invalid on upgrade.
- If the old config also had telemetry enabled, doctor disables `diagnostics.otel.enabled` while removing `protocol`, preserving the old runtime behavior where `grpc` never exported telemetry.
- `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc` remains a fail-closed runtime override path, and now has focused regression coverage.
- Real gRPC exporter support is intentionally out of scope here.

## Linked context

Closes #93069.

Related review concern: ClawSweeper flagged the first version because it rejected a shipped config value without an upgrade path. This revision adds that doctor migration path and proof.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Existing `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` configs no longer get stranded by the stricter schema; doctor removes the unsupported protocol and disables enabled telemetry until the operator chooses an OTLP/HTTP collector.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node v25.6.1, branch head `074f6a0b45`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '
  const { validateConfigObjectRaw } = await import("./src/config/validation.ts");
  const { migrateLegacyConfig } = await import("./src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.ts");
  const input = { diagnostics: { otel: { enabled: true, endpoint: "http://otel-collector:4317", protocol: "grpc" } } };
  const before = validateConfigObjectRaw(input);
  const migrated = migrateLegacyConfig(input);
  const after = validateConfigObjectRaw(migrated.config ?? {});
  console.log("before.ok=" + before.ok);
  if (!before.ok) console.log("before.issue=" + before.issues.find((issue) => issue.path === "diagnostics.otel.protocol")?.message);
  console.log("migration.changes=" + JSON.stringify(migrated.changes));
  console.log("after.config=" + JSON.stringify(migrated.config?.diagnostics?.otel));
  console.log("after.ok=" + after.ok);
'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
before.ok=false
before.issue=Invalid input: expected "http/protobuf" (allowed: "http/protobuf")
migration.changes=["Removed unsupported diagnostics.otel.protocol \"grpc\"; use \"http/protobuf\" with an OTLP/HTTP collector.","Disabled diagnostics.otel.enabled because legacy grpc configs never exported telemetry; re-enable it after choosing an OTLP/HTTP collector."]
after.config={"enabled":false,"endpoint":"http://otel-collector:4317"}
after.ok=true
```

- Observed result after fix: The unsupported `grpc` config is rejected by validation before migration, doctor migration removes `protocol`, disables enabled telemetry, keeps the endpoint intact, and the migrated config validates successfully.
- What was not tested: A live OTLP collector or real telemetry export; real gRPC exporter support is not implemented by this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The direct proof imports the actual config validation and doctor migration modules from the source checkout. The runtime env override path is covered by the focused diagnostics-otel service test below rather than by a live collector run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper’s earlier review reproduced the issue by source inspection and flagged the lack of a doctor/migration path for shipped `grpc` configs.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/deprecation-compat.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test-utils.ts src/config/validation.allowed-values.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
  - Passed: 5 Vitest shards, 343 tests.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed.

Regression coverage added:

- Doctor migration removes `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` and disables enabled telemetry.
- Doctor preflight reports shipped `grpc` configs as repairable legacy config.
- Doctor config flow applies the repair end to end.
- Config validation reports the allowed value as `http/protobuf`.
- Diagnostics OTel service rejects `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc` before exporter startup.
- Deprecation compatibility inventory tracks the migration so it is not deleted accidentally.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — unsupported `grpc` config now fails validation, with doctor repair for existing configs.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — schema narrows to `http/protobuf`, and doctor migrates legacy `grpc` configs.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: upgrade behavior for existing `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` configs. The doctor migration is intentionally conservative: it preserves the old no-export outcome by disabling telemetry when the unsupported `grpc` config had been enabled.
target.author: kiranmagic7

commands.author: kiranmagic7
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs human/contributor follow-up for real behavior proof and PR scope framing; no narrow automated code repair is identified.; Cleared: The diff adds CLI/docs caveat text and tests only; it does not change exec authorization, approvals resolution, dependencies, CI, secrets, or package execution paths. (sha=2af384fa1ffc99294f124ddfa29ba551b026faf7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds CLI/docs notes and tests clarifying that `openclaw exec-policy show` and `openclaw approvals get` exclude per-session `/exec` overrides.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +27, Docs +13. Total +50 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level diagnostic gap: current main's CLI notes omit the per-session `/exec` caveat, and current source/docs show `/exec` with no arguments is the session-specific status path. I did not establish a live terminal or chat-session run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human/contributor follow-up for real behavior proof and PR scope framing; no narrow automated code repair is identified.
